content.problemStatement:
Chef has a grid gridgridgrid filled with non-negative numbers and wants to find a path from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner that minimizes the sum of all numbers along the path. 

Chef can only move either down or right at any point in time. Help Chef determine the minimum possible sum for such a path.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateMinimumPathSumcalculateMinimumPathSumcalculateMinimumPathSum - This function calculates the minimum path sum from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of a grid by moving only down or right.

Parameters
gridgridgrid : A 2D matrix representing the grid of non-negative integers.
Each element in numberGridnumberGridnumberGrid represents the cost or value at that grid cell.
Return Value
Returns a number representing the minimum sum path value.
The return value is the smallest sum of numbers along a path from the top-left to bottom-right cell.
The path can only move either down or right at any point in time.
Constraints
1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤rowCount≤2001 \leq rowCount \leq 2001≤rowCount≤200
0≤numberGrid[i][j]≤2000 \leq numberGrid[i][j] \leq 2000≤numberGrid[i][j]≤200
The gridgridgrid can be either rectangle or square.

Input Format

First line contains the TTT - test cases.
The first line contains rowCountrowCountrowCount — the number rows in Chef's grid.
The next rowCountrowCountrowCount lines each contain integers representing the values in gridgridgrid.


Output Format

Print a single integer — the minimum sum of numbers along a path from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid, moving only down or right.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 200), the number of rows in the grid. The next N lines each contain N space-separated non-negative integers representing the grid values.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer — the minimum sum of numbers along a path from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid, moving only down or right.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: path 1→4→5 gives sum 10, which is minimal. Test case 2: the optimal path 10→1→10→1→10 yields sum 32.
First test case all zeros, so minimum sum is 0. Second test case the cheapest path is 7→1→0→0 with sum 9.
Single cell grid, the only possible path sum equals the cell value 42.

solution.approach: This problem can be solved using dynamic programming. The idea is to maintain a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the minimum sum of numbers along a path from the top-left to the cell at position (i, j) in the grid. We can calculate dp[i][j] by taking the minimum of dp[i-1][j] and dp[i][j-1] and adding the value of the current cell grid[i][j]. We start by initializing the first row and the first column of the dp array, and then we fill in the rest of the array. Finally, the minimum sum of numbers along a path from the top-left to the bottom-right corner of the grid is stored in dp[N-1][N-1].
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) per test case, O(T * N^2) total. The time complexity is quadratic because we need to fill in the dp array, which has N^2 cells.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the dp array.
